MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-01-2020 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ALICE & OLIVIA CALIFORNIA HOLDINGS LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED 5 DAYS TO ANSWER, SUBJECT TO AGREEMENT FROM THE OTHER SIDE TO CONTINUE THE HEARING. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHOW THAT ANY PROVISIONS OF THE COMMERCIAL EVICTION MORATORIUM PRECLUDE THE FILING OF A CIVIL ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT OR IMPOSE ANY LIMITATIONS ON SUCH FILINGS. FURTHERMORE, DEFENDANTS FAILED TO SHOW THAT ANYTHING ON THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT INDICATES THAT DEFENDANTS ARE NOT IN BREACH OF LEASE PER THE TERMS OF THE LEASE OR UNDER SAN MATEO COMMUNITY V. HALF MOON BAY (1998) 65 CAL.APP.4TH 401 AND PETROLEUM COLLECTIONS V. SWORDS (1975) 48 CAL.APP.3D 841. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(501/REQ)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-02-2022 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-15-2021 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-13-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
Off Calendar Feb-26-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jonathan Seigal; Scheer Law Group, LLP; 415-491-8900; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiffs Balgobind Jaiswal and Mehri Jaiswal.
Noah Balch; Katten Muchin Rosenman LLP; 310-788-4516; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendant Alice & Olivia California Holdings LLC.

Court Reporter: Jane Hong, CSR# 11975, csrjane@hotmail.com

Defendant Alice & Olivia California Holdings LLC contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF BALGOBIND JAISWAL, MEHRI JAISWAL APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER is GRANTED as requested. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court Reporter: Jane Hong, CSR# 11975, csrjane@hotmail.com (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-27-2021 Set For Trial On Sep-27-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-14-2020 Continued To Feb-10-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Court Call):
Noah Balch, Esq, 310-788-4516 of Katten Muchin Rosenman LLP for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D. Counsel requests to continue the motion so he can contest the Court's ruling properly. The Court denies the the request to continue without Prejudice and will delay signing the order for a couple of days. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ALICE & OLIVIA CALIFORNIA HOLDINGS LLC DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Subject to agreement from the prevailing party to continue the hearing for argument. The Court will adopt its tentative ruling as uncontested and will execute an order no later than Dec. 30, 2020. Demurrer is OVERRULED 5 days to answer. Moving party failed to show that any provisions of the Commercial Eviction Moratorium preclude the filing of a civil action for breach of contract or impose any limitations on such filings. Furthermore, Defendants failed to show that anything on the face of the complaint indicates that defendants are not in breach of lease per the terms of the lease or under San Mateo Community v. Half Moon Bay (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 401 and Petroleum Collections v. Swords (1975) 48 Cal.App.3d 841.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. =(501/REQ)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2020 Continued To Dec-21-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ALICE & OLIVIA CALIFORNIA HOLDINGS LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Matter is transferred to be heard in department 501 on December 21, 2020 at 9:30 a.m. That department hears all breach of lease cases per SF Local Rule 8.10A1.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
